title: Treatment of Spaces
concept: By treating spaces as standard characters ('_'), SentencePiece can train and tokenize languages that don't use spaces between words (like Chinese or Japanese) natively.

exercises.prompt: How does SentencePiece represent spaces so it can treat them like normal letters?
exercises.explain: SentencePiece treats spaces as standard characters (like an underscore '_'), which allows it to process all languages consistently.

cards.title: The Missing Spaces
cards.body: How do you teach a computer to read a language like Chinese or Japanese that doesn't use spaces between words? If the computer only knows how to split words by finding spaces, it gets completely stuck! Let's find out how we solve this.

cards.title: What is 'Treatment of Spaces'?
cards.body: Traditional tokenizers split text by looking for blank spaces. SentencePiece does something different: it treats spaces as regular characters, representing them with a special symbol (like '_'). This means it doesn't need spaces to decide where to cut words!

cards.title: The Train of Characters
cards.body: Imagine a long train where all the cars are hooked together, but some cars are special passenger cars and others are empty flatbeds. SentencePiece doesn't see spaces as invisible boundaries that break the train apart; it just sees them as flatbed cars (written as ' '). Every car, whether a word or a space, is treated as a solid piece of the same train.

cards.title: Spaces in Action
cards.body: In English, the phrase "Hello World" is treated by SentencePiece as "_Hello_World". In Japanese, a phrase without spaces like "こんにちは世界" is processed in the exact same way, without needing any spaces to be added first. Both are just strings of characters!

cards.title: Myth: Spaces are Lost Forever
cards.body: You might think that because SentencePiece replaces spaces with a special symbol, it changes or ruins the original text. In fact, because the space is preserved as a character, the computer can translate the tokens back into the exact original text, spaces and all, with zero loss!

cards.title: Comparing Tokenizers
cards.body: See how traditional tokenizers break when there are no spaces, while SentencePiece handles both types of languages effortlessly.

right.text: Treats spaces as regular '_' characters. It works perfectly on both English and Japanese without needing space boundaries.

left.text: Splits text using spaces. It gets confused by languages like Japanese because it cannot find any spaces to split on.

exercises.prompt: Which of these languages benefits the most from SentencePiece's treatment of spaces because it doesn't traditionally use spaces between words?
exercises.explain: Because Chinese does not use spaces to separate words, traditional space-based tokenizers fail. SentencePiece works because it treats spaces like any other character and doesn't rely on them to split text.

exercises.prompt: True or False: SentencePiece cannot tokenize languages like Japanese because it strictly requires spaces between words.
exercises.explain: SentencePiece treats spaces as normal characters, allowing it to easily handle languages that do not use spaces.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the explanation of SentencePiece's space handling.
exercises.sentence: SentencePiece solves the spacing problem by treating a space as a standard character.
exercises.explain: By treating the space as a standard character (often displayed as '_'), the tokenizer doesn't need to treat spaces differently from letters.

exercises.prompt: How does SentencePiece handle spaces during tokenization?

options.text: It treats spaces as standard characters using a special symbol, making it easy to reconstruct the exact input.
It automatically deletes all spaces from the text because computers do not need spaces to read.
It replaces every single space with a random letter from the alphabet to confuse hackers.

exercises.explain: AI 1 is correct because SentencePiece treats spaces as a regular character (often represented by a special symbol like a mini-underscore) to keep track of them.

exercises.prompt: Why is the 'space-as-character' approach in SentencePiece considered a major breakthrough for multilingual AI models?

options.text: It ignores spaces to save computer memory.
It forces all languages to add spaces between their characters.
It treats spaces as standard characters ('_'), keeping the process identical for all languages.
It deletes all spaces and replaces them with punctuation marks.

exercises.explain: Treating spaces as regular '_' characters allows SentencePiece to handle all languages (with or without spaces) using the exact same code, and permits reconstruction of the exact original text.
